5:0.note: Robustness pass on the new batch

suite.pass: 1. Quick check — do this every build

items.h: Open the game. It should show up normally — no error pop-ups, no blank/black screen.
items.t: Nothing is broken when it opens

items.h: From the page loading, it should take one click or tap (or none) to actually be playing. No menus in the way.
Play a complete run/level/match from start to finish without getting stuck.
items.t: You can finish one whole round

items.h: After you finish or lose, you can cleanly start again.
You can hear audio, and pressing mute actually silences it.
No hanging, no forever-loading, no screen you can’t get out of.

suite.pass: 2. First-timer test — do this when something is new

items.h: Pretend you’ve never seen it. Can you work out what to do just by looking at the screen?
Write down anywhere you got confused. That’s a spot to fix — it’s not you being slow.
items.t: No “wait… what do I do?” moments

items.h: Try things it might not expect — spam buttons, tap random spots. It shouldn’t break.
At any moment you can tell: what to do now, what you just did, and what’s coming next.
items.t: You always know where you are

suite.pass: 3. Check every feature — the thorough pass

items.h: Click each button/menu item. It should do exactly what its label promises.
items.t: Every button does what it says

items.h: Try keyboard, mouse/touch, and controller (if supported). Each should reach every action.
Earn some, spend some. Check the numbers change correctly and don’t glitch.
Close the game and reopen it. Whatever you unlocked or earned should still be there.
Check the edges: having zero of something, the maximum, and the very first time vs. later times.
Resize the window and try a phone-shaped screen. Nothing should overflow or get cut off.

suite.pass: 4. Is it fun and fair? — play a few rounds

items.h: Do 3–5 full sessions in a row so you feel the real rhythm, not just one lucky or unlucky run.
items.t: Played a few rounds back to back

items.h: Note any moment that felt cheap or unfairly hard, and roughly where it happened.
Note any stretch that dragged or felt repetitive. Boredom counts as a real problem here.
Would you start a 4th or 5th round? Jot down why or why not — that answer matters most.
Rate fun / fair / clear / would-recommend, each 1–5. Do it each build so you can see the trend.

suite.pass: 5. Ready for the store — do this before you submit

items.h: Run the platform’s own checklist. For CrazyGames that’s GAME_BIBLE.md in the play-area repo.
Not just your desktop browser — try it on an actual phone and/or tablet.
Try it with an ad-blocker on, slow internet, a backgrounded tab, and rotating the phone.

suite.pass: 6. Did a fix break anything? — do this after fixing bugs

items.h: Re-check everything near what you changed, not only the one bug you were chasing.
Do pass 1 again fully. A fix that breaks the basics is worse than the bug it fixed.
If you changed something lots of features rely on, test all of those features, not just one.

suite.pass: 7. New this build — DR-#025→#032 (Grabber, reabsorb, drag-paint, sell, rot, gates)

items.h: Pick GRABBER (45◈), place one on a lane. Watch enemies cross its amber aura ring — they should visibly CRAWL while inside it and speed back up once they leave.

items.t: GRABBER looks distinct from a ZOMBIE
items.h: A grabber should be unmistakable next to a normal zombie: amber (not lime) base disc, a dashed amber ring showing its slow radius, and wiggling grasping claw-arms. You should tell them apart at a glance.
A grabber only slows — it should never fire an acid shot. Only the ZOMBIE bites/shoots.

items.t: Reabsorption cooldown fires when a unit dies
items.h: Let enemies kill one of your zombies/grabbers. Right after it dies, try to place a NEW zombie or grabber: it should be REFUSED with a 'REABSORBING Ns' message, and both unit buttons dim + count down ~4s. After the countdown you can build units again.

items.t: Cooldown does NOT block hedges or salvage
items.h: While the 'reabsorbing' countdown is running: you should STILL be able to place HEDGE walls, and selling your own unit yourself (SALVAGE) should NOT start the cooldown.
Pick HEDGE, then press and DRAG across several tiles in one motion — a whole wall should appear in one stroke (not one tap per tile). Zombies/grabbers should still be one tap each (no drag-spam).

With a brush selected, hover over the field: a filled ghost square shows where it'll drop — green (in the brush's color) where it's allowed, RED with an X where it isn't (on the hive, a gate, when you can't afford it, or where it would pinch the path).

Tap one of YOUR hedges with no brush active → a REMOVE button appears and refunds a few ◈. Tap a starting (seeded) hedge → REMOVE says 'free'. Tap a zombie/grabber → SALVAGE refunds biomass. The lane should re-open after removal.

Build a long winding maze. Enemies taking the long way should take steadily INCREASING green 'rot' damage the longer they're inside (green motes rising off them) — a long maze should visibly wear the horde down vs a short one.
At wave 2 the WEST gate should flash/flare with a banner 'THE WEST GATE OPENS'. At wave 5 the NORTH & SOUTH gates flare together. You should clearly SEE on the field which new entrance just opened.
Hedges should read as leafy bushes — dappled light/dark green clumps with a lit top edge — not flat green slabs. Judge if it 'pops' at real size.

items.t: Boons & text match the current loop
items.h: When 'THE HIVE ADAPTS' offers boons between waves, they should mention things you can actually build (Zombie / Grabber / Hedge — e.g. GRASPING REACH, CHOKEHOLD, THORNED HEDGES). No leftover 'Spore/Rootmass/mutate a corpse' wording anywhere (onboarding hint should say pick a brush & tap the field, not 'tap a corpse').

Play a full run start to finish: waves spawn from the right gates, biomass in/out is correct, pause/mute/speed toggle, death + endless all still work (a big chunk of dead code was removed this build — make sure the basics are intact).

suite.pass: 7. New this build — DR-#042→#045 (Levels · Queen doorway · evolve-to-Grabber · paused modals · top ticker)

items.t: Level 1 starts you building around the Queen
items.h: New run opens on LEVEL 1 — THE BURROW GATE. The Queen is already placed for you at the deep spot (you no longer place her). You build the dungeon of zombies/spikes AROUND her.

items.t: Only ZOMBIE and SPIKES to build
items.h: The build palette should show just two options — ZOMBIE and SPIKES. No doors, no hedges, no separate GRABBER button (grabber now comes from evolving a zombie).

items.t: Survive the waves → the Queen opens the doorway
items.h: Clear the level's last wave. You should get a paused 'THE QUEEN OPENS THE WAY' beat, the east exit arch flares purple, your surviving units are refunded as biomass, then the next level begins with a fresh build phase and restored hive HP.

items.t: You can see where the exit will open
items.h: All level long a faint sealed arch is drawn on the east wall, so you always know which way you're heading before it actually opens.

items.t: Evolve a ZOMBIE into a GRABBER
items.h: Tap one of your placed zombies → EVOLVE → GRABBER (30◈). It keeps its slow aura and gains a green 'rot' damage-over-time (~2.5/s) on anything inside the aura. A one-time teach banner should explain it on your first zombie.

items.t: Grabber looks like the SAME art pack
items.h: An evolved grabber should read as the same zombie model but amber-tinted with arms out/grasping (the pack's ROAR pose) — clearly the same art family as the green walking zombie, NOT a mismatched blob.

items.t: Idle zombies roam, then freeze on prey
items.h: Zombies with nothing to fight should shamble a little (under ~1 tile) around their post. The instant a raider comes into range they should snap still and attack.

items.t: Story beats PAUSE the game (no overlap)
items.h: Level intros, wave-start beats (THE SCOUTS / PURGE / BUTCHER / etc.) and 'THE QUEEN OPENS THE WAY' should DIM and PAUSE — the raid must NOT spawn until you tap or press Enter to dismiss. Nothing should print over the live action.

items.t: Small messages ride a slim top ticker
items.h: In-play notices (enemy first-seen, ambush, wave repelled, ability activations, reabsorb refund) should appear as a slim pill docked at the very top rock row — one at a time, no screen dim, never covering the field.
The adventurer raid should behave like real players — picking targets, splitting at junctions, fighting back — not just walking a single line into the hive.
Play through Level 1 → 2 → 3 to the victory screen. NOTE: L2/L3 aren't fully balance-tuned yet, so log where a difficulty spike feels UNFAIR rather than treating a loss as a bug.
One full run: biomass earns/spends correctly, pause (P) / mute (M) / speed toggle all work, and death → restart is clean.
